title: The Dunning-Kruger Effect
observation: Individuals with low ability at a task overestimate their own competence.
effect: A lack of self-awareness regarding one's own limitations creates a false sense of expertise.

title: Pareidolia
observation: Seeing human faces, animals, or specific shapes in random patterns or inanimate objects.
effect: The brain’s evolutionary need to identify potential threats or social cues misinterprets neutral data.

title: The Baader-Meinhof Phenomenon
observation: Noticing something frequently soon after first encountering it, leading to the belief it is appearing everywhere.
effect: Selective attention and confirmation bias make the brain prioritize new information in its surroundings.

title: Survivorship Bias
observation: Focusing only on the people or things that 'survived' a process while ignoring the failures.
effect: Drawing erroneous conclusions because the data set is incomplete due to missing negative results.

title: The Zeigarnik Effect
observation: The tendency to remember uncompleted or interrupted tasks better than completed ones.
effect: The brain maintains cognitive tension to ensure the task remains in working memory until finalized.
